rationale: Both outputs correctly frame the contribution as computational, preserve the optimum, bound the result to the tested instances, and identify an evidence-dependent operational pathway to profit. B is preferable because it retains the supported wording that the instances solved 40% faster. A changes this to a 40% reduction in solution time, which is not mathematically equivalent and introduces an unsupported empirical magnitude. A is slightly more economical, but B has stronger numerical fidelity and evidence discipline while remaining concise.
